Other, please specify: a. Knee (LT) C. f. d. b. g. e. C. h. f. d. i. e. j. Sx: 4/3/26 Clinical findings or services required ins support to patient's home health eligibility: Skilled Nursing Physcial Therapy / Occupational Therapy Speech Language Pathology Skilled evaluation/treatment: Health education on disease process Gait/balance/transfer training & optimization - Medication reconciliation/management Home safety /fall precaution - Aphasia Dysarthria DM management Passive and/or Active ROM Exercises - Apraxia Dysphagia Catheter/Drain care - Post op exercises Post operative care Education for safe use of assistive device Medical Social Worker - - Wound Care and pt/family training Set up home exercise program Ostomy care and pt/family training Therapeutic exercise for strengthening & I - Psychosocial assessment, Central line care endurance dentification or assistance in O2 Therapy/Trache management Others: - I application for community Others: government resources Others: - Tel #: 702.258.5567 Signature: Ordering Provider: Date: 3/25/26 Address: NEVADA ORTHOPEDIC & SPINE CENTER 7455 W. Washington Ave Suite 160 Phone: (702) 878-0393 1505 Wigwam Pkwy Suite 330 Las Vegas, Nevada 89128 www.nevadaorthopedic.com Henderson, Nevada 89074 Timmothy Boyer MRN#: 3140510 DOB: 03/12/1956, 70 years Date: 02/03/2026 Orthopedic Evaluation History of Present Illness The patient is a 69 year old male who presents for an evaluation of left knee. Additional reasons for visit: Transition into care is described as the following: The patient is transitioning into care from another physician and a summary of care was not provided. This patient presents to clinic today with complaints of left knee pain. Pain is localized to the medial aspect of the knee. Pain is sharp with activity and aching at rest. He has attempted therapeutic exercise but it was too painful to continue. He has used nonsteroidal anti-inflammatories and they did not provide relief. He cannot walk more than 200 m at a time due to knee pain. He has difficulty ascending or descending stairs due to the knee pain. Examination: Head, ears, eyes, nose, and throat: Atraumatic and normocephalic. Alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation. Skin: No open lesions, rashes or wounds. No café-au-lait spots. No dark or hairy patches present. Vascular: Normal distal pulses all limbs. Capillary refill less than 2 seconds all limbs. No adenopathy present. No stasis changes about ankles. Normal finger and toenails. Neurologic: Cranial nerves II XII grossly intact. Light touch intact all limbs. Motor exam is 5/5 upper and lower extremities. Gait pattern is antalgic toward the left bearing full weight. Hip: Limb lengths are symmetric. Nontender over the greater trochanter. No flexion contracture present. Nontender about either buttock, thigh or knee to palpation. Nontender about the lumbar spine. Negative straight leg raise. Left knee examination shows that there is a flexible 5 degree varus deformity. There is medial joint line tenderness. No crepitus. No effusion present. Radiographs of the left knee were obtained and are reviewed with the patient. Medial compartment arthritis with a varus deformity. There is osteophytes medially. No pathology in the patellofemoral joint or in the lateral compartment. Impression: Left knee medial compartment arthritis. Severe. The remaining compartments are uninvolved. Recommendation and medical decision making: Patient is advised in this setting he is a good candidate for partial knee replacement. We discussed the indications risks and benefits. We discussed the recovery timeline. In anticipation of surgery I am ordering a CT scan for the planning protocol for the Mako knee. Follow-up for preop. Reviewed HPI, History and ROS at today's visit. The dictated information, contained herein, was transcribed with voice recognition software. Although reviewed, the content may contain errors and/or omissions. Printed: 03/25/2026 12:19:51 PM Page 1 / 3 Patient History Allergies No known drug allergy Allergies Reconciled Medications simvastatin (20mg tablet, oral) Active. Past Surgical Knee Surgery Phreesia 01/28/2026 Social Alcohol Assessment: No Current Alcohol Problem 12/18/2024 Alcohol use: Occasional alcohol use 12/18/2024 Assistance Status: Home Assistance Available 12/18/2024 Contract: No Active Pain Contract 12/18/2024 Current Rehab Status: No Current Drug Rehab 12/18/2024 Current work status: Not Currently Employed 12/18/2024 Disability Status: Not Disabled 12/18/2024 Drug Assessment: No Ilicit Drug Use 12/18/2024 Drug Dependency: No Drug Dependence 12/18/2024 Exercise: daily 12/18/2024 Hand Dominance: Left Handed, Right or Left Handed 12/18/2024 Past Rehab Status: No Past Drug Rehab 12/18/2024 Stair Tolerance: 5+ Flights 12/18/2024 Surgical Blood Transfusion: Approves surgical blood transfusion 12/18/2024 Tobacco use: Never smoker 12/18/2024 Other Past Medical No pertinent past medical history Phreesia 01/28/2026 Problem List Status post right knee replacement Problems Reconciled Vitals 02/03/2026 09:38 AM Weight: 201 lb Height: 71 in Body Mass Index: 28.03 kg/m² Pain Level: 5/10 "BMI >25: Losing Weight" handout given to address weight-loss options and benefits. Review of Systems General Not Present- Chills, Fever, Night Sweats, Persistent Infections, Recurring Infections, Weight Gain and Weight Loss. HEENT Present- Wears glasses/contact lenses. Not Present- Hearing Loss, Hoarseness and Visual Disturbances. Cardiovascular Not Present- Chest Pain, Difficulty Breathing On Exertion, Palpitations, Shortness of Breath and Swelling of Extremities. Neurological Not Present- Burning, Decreased Memory, Difficulty Using Hands, Numbness, Seizures, Stroke, Tingling and Trouble walking. Psychiatric Not Present- Anxiety, Depression, Fearful, Frequent crying and Other Psychological Condition. Hematology Not Present- Easy Bruising and Excessive bleeding.
